What is the role of the hormone adrenaline in the 'fight or flight' response?

options.B: To increase heart rate and blood pressure

questions.question: What is the term for the phenomenon where the binding of one hormone to its receptor affects the binding of another hormone to its receptor?
Which of the following endocrine glands is responsible for the production of the hormone parathyroid hormone (PTH)?
What is the primary function of the hormone somatostatin in the human body?

options.A: To regulate the production of growth hormone
options.B: To regulate the production of thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH)
options.C: To regulate the production of insulin
options.D: To regulate the production of glucagon

questions.question: Which hormone, secreted by the adrenal medulla, is responsible for the 'fight or flight' response?
questions.hint: This catecholamine is secreted in response to stress.
questions.solution: The adrenal medulla secretes adrenaline (epinephrine) and noradrenaline as part of the sympathetic stress response, which increases heart rate, dilates pupils, and redirects blood to muscles.
